title: Job switch leads to ₹1.69 lakh tax liability despite both employers deducting TDS correctly: Here's why Form 12B matters
summary: Switching jobs during a financial year can leave salaried employees with an unexpected tax liability even if both employers deducted TDS correctly. The gap arises because TDS is calculated employer-wise, while final tax is based on total annual income. Form 12B can help avoid this mismatch.

title: How to lock and unlock your fingerprints and iris using new Aadhaar App
summary: The Aadhaar biometric lock/unlock feature has gained significant adoption, with residents using it more than 1.91 crore times, according to MeitY. This security feature allows Aadhaar holders to prevent unauthorized biometric authentication using their fingerprints, iris and face data.

title: The NRI guilt trap: why high salaries abroad don't always create wealth
summary: Many NRIs earn in dollars but struggle to build wealth as family obligations, emotional guilt and social expectations drain savings and delay financial independence.

title: The coaching race is costing parents lakhs. Here's how to plan
summary: Parents are spending ₹2-4 lakh on coaching before college admissions. With education inflation at 10-12%, experts warn against dipping into retirement savings or taking on debt.

title: National Student Laptop Scheme 2026 is fake: PIB Fact Check reiterates warning against viral WhatsApp message
summary: PIB Fact Check has again debunked a viral WhatsApp message, first flagged in January, claiming the Government is accepting applications for free laptops under the ‘National Student Laptop Scheme 2026’. It reiterated that no such scheme exists.

title: Hotels can't add a service charge to your bill without informing you: CCPA acts against 41 restaurants
summary: CCPA has initiated action against 41 restaurants for automatically adding service charges to customers' bills, calling it an unfair trade practice. The move reinforces that service charges are voluntary, and restaurants must not levy them by default or without clearly informing consumers.

title: Automatic EPF transfer after changing jobs: Things to check before your balance is moved to the new account
summary: EPFO has introduced automatic PF transfers for eligible members, simplifying the job-switch process. Here are the key things to check before your balance is moved to the new account.

title: Attention taxpayers! Here's what you should do after receiving a notice from the I-T department — Stepwise guide

title: Can forming a HUF help you save tax? Experts explain who can create one, key benefits and what to consider
summary: Forming a Hindu Undivided Family (HUF) can help taxpayers manage taxes efficiently as it is a separate taxable entity under income tax law, however a family must understand how it works first. Here's what experts say.

title: Gifting to your NRI child? Know the tax treatment of NRE deposits
summary: Gifts from parents to NRI children are tax-free, while interest earned on NRE accounts and deposits is fully exempt from income tax for FEMA non-residents, unlike taxable NRO interest.

title: India 10-year bond slumps to lowest in nearly a month as Middle East oil risks heat up
summary: Indian government bonds experienced a significant decline early Monday. Escalating U.S.-Iran tensions pushed oil prices higher, impacting the rupee. This situation prompted traders to reduce their positions in government bonds. Higher crude prices strain India's import bill and government finances.

title: Warren Buffett admits to a rare mistake with these 2 big tech stock bets
summary: Warren Buffett admits he bought Alphabet too late and sold Apple too soon, even as Apple remains Berkshire's largest holding at nearly 22% of its portfolio. The 95-year-old also warns that today's market favours gambling over value investing.

title: India Inc is returning to banks for money, but retail borrowers aren’t keeping pace
summary: India’s largest private banks are witnessing a shift in credit demand, with corporate and SME lending driving loan growth while retail borrowing remains subdued. title: US equity funds see first weekly outflow in three weeks as chip selloff weighs on sentiment
summary: US equity funds saw their first weekly outflow in three weeks, with a net $4.8 billion pulled amid a semiconductor selloff and US-Iran tensions, even as bond funds extended their winning streak to 13 weeks and money market funds recorded their largest outflow since April, per LSEG Lipper data cited
